expectations.text: With-skill subagent spawned 3 separate Codex panes via swarm_spawn_agent (NOT mcp__codex__codex)
expectations.evidence: PANES_SPAWNED: %56 %57 %58. All 3 via swarm_spawn_agent. Subagent explicitly avoided mcp__codex__codex per the HARD RULE.

expectations.text: Each Codex pane received a different task file via swarm_pipe_prompt
All 3 .result files were written with Status: DONE
expectations.evidence: RESULT_STATUSES: DONE/DONE/DONE. All 3 .result files exist at /tmp/rocky-eval-codex-parallel/.swarm/sess-test-cx/tasks/

expectations.text: All 3 module files exist: projects/fib/fib.py, projects/fact/fact.py, projects/isprime/isprime.py
fib(10) returns 55 in the implementation (functional verification)
expectations.evidence: Subagent ran functional verification: fib(10) = 55 PASS

expectations.text: isprime(17) returns True in the implementation
All 3 panes were killed on cleanup

claims.claim: swarm_poll_result expects the task .md path, not the .result path
claims.evidence: Subagent discovered this during the test: 'swarm_poll_result takes the task .md path (not the .result path) — calling it with the .result path returns ERROR: task file not found immediately. Once corrected, polling succeeded with exit 0 for all three.' The Phase 3.3 reference correctly uses the .md path; the docs are right but the eval prompt was wrong.

claims.claim: Tiled layout produces a 2x2 grid for orchestrator + 3 Codex coders
claims.evidence: TILE_CALL_EXECUTED_AFTER_SPAWN: yes. The 2x2 grid was visible in tmux and the user did not complain about layout for this test (unlike the prior 3-column rejection)

suggestions.reason: The eval prompt I (the orchestrator) wrote had the wrong file extension for swarm_poll_result. Fix the eval prompt to use .md not .result. Also worth adding an assertion that swarm_poll_result accepts a .md path so future tests don't repeat the mistake.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong eval, well-grounded. The MCP-vs-swarm distinction is the headline finding — baseline used MCP (rocky-forbidden), with-skill used the proper swarm bash transport.
